name: qm-level-of-theory
description: Choosing a QM functional/basis: run `qm recommend <task>` (general | barriers | nci | thermochemistry) for the curated level before picking by hand.
caveats: `qm recommend` is read-only and needs no structure; use it first, then run the actual qm job.
Composites (r2scan-3c, b97-3c, pbeh-3c, b3lyp-3c) bundle their own basis + dispersion - do NOT pass --basis with them.
def2-svp (the qm default basis) is only a lower bound for geometries; use >= def2-tzvp for energies.
body: The cost/accuracy ladder SilicoLab can run: composites (r2scan-3c all-rounder; b97-3c structures/freq; pbeh-3c for SIE-prone/polar systems and TS guesses) -> hybrids (pbe0, b3lyp) -> range-separated hybrids (wb97m-v, wb97x-v; best for barriers and non-covalent interactions) -> double-hybrid (b2plyp, needs a large/QZ basis) -> wavefunction (mp2, ccsd, ccsd(t) for small references). Always keep dispersion on (wb97m-v/wb97x-v carry VV10; otherwise add a -d4 suffix or --dispersion d4). NOT available here: semi-empirical xtb/GFN, DLPNO-CCSD(T), W1/W2, and automated conformer search (CREST/CENSO) - those are external tools.

name: qm-geometry-and-conformers
description: Optimize a molecule / find a minimum: r2scan-3c is the all-round first choice. Optimize then run freq to confirm a true minimum.
caveats: r2scan-3c carries its own triple-zeta basis - do NOT pass --basis.
`qm optimize` creates a NEW optimized entry and activates it; the following `qm freq`/`qm energy` then act on that optimized entry.
SilicoLab has no built-in conformer search; build or import important conformers yourself.
body: b97-3c is a cheaper triple-zeta alternative for structures and frequencies; pbeh-3c (small basis) suits SIE-prone/polar systems and transition-state guesses but carries some BSSE. Imaginary frequencies from `qm freq` mean the geometry is not a minimum (or is a saddle point) - re-optimize.

name: qm-thermochemistry
description: Reaction / formation / Gibbs free energies: optimize+freq at r2scan-3c, then a wb97m-v/def2-tzvpp single point (matches `qm recommend thermochemistry`).
caveats: `qm energy` is the ~0 K electronic energy; the `qm freq` run adds ZPE + thermal (mRRHO) corrections - combine as G = E_high + (G_low - E_low).
Set --spin (2S+1) for open-shell species and --charge for ions; build each species and `activate` it before its job.
Set --symmetry-number for symmetric molecules so the rotational entropy is right.
body: This is the standard composite free-energy protocol: cheap geometry + thermal corrections at r2scan-3c, accurate electronic energy from the range-separated hybrid. r2scan-3c opt+freq alone already gives decent thermochemistry; the wb97m-v single point is the accuracy refinement. Run `qm recommend thermochemistry` for the engine's exact recommendation and notes.

name: qm-barriers-kinetics
description: Reaction barriers / transition states: locate the saddle with `qm ts`, then take a range-separated-hybrid single point on it (`qm recommend barriers`).
caveats: GGAs and composites systematically underestimate barriers - use a (range-separated) hybrid for the energy.

`qm ts` runs the saddle search at a composite/hybrid with an analytic gradient (e.g. r2scan-3c, b3lyp); the RS-hybrid (wb97m-v) energy then refines the barrier on that geometry. Confirm the saddle with `--irc` or a `qm freq` showing exactly one imaginary mode.
body: Use `qm ts` to climb to the first-order saddle: from a near-saddle geometry, between a reactant and a product (`--product`), or along a driven coordinate (`--scan-bond`/`--scan-angle`/`--scan-dihedral`). wb97m-v (with VV10 nonlocal correlation) is among the most accurate functionals for kinetics but has no analytic gradient, so run the search at r2scan-3c/b3lyp and take the wb97m-v energy on the located saddle.

name: qm-noncovalent
description: Non-covalent interaction energies (pi-stacking, H-bonds, vdW): wb97m-v with a triple-zeta basis on a r2scan-3c geometry; dispersion must be on (`qm recommend nci`).
caveats: Dispersion is essential for NCI - wb97m-v includes VV10; for other functionals add a -d4 suffix.
Basis-set superposition error matters; use >= def2-tzvpp and consider it when comparing complexes.
body: For interaction energies of a complex, single-point both the complex and the separated fragments at the same level and take the difference. A larger basis (def2-tzvppd / def2-qzvp) reduces BSSE further at higher cost.

name: qm-anions-and-diffuse
description: Anions, dipole moments, polarizabilities, electron affinities: add diffuse functions - use a ma-def2 / def2...D basis such as ma-def2-tzvp.
caveats: Without diffuse functions, anion energies and electron-rich properties are unreliable.
Match --charge and --spin to the actual ion.
body: Diffuse-augmented bases (ma-def2-TZVP, def2-TZVPD) describe the loosely bound electron density of anions and the response of dipoles/polarizabilities. For optical/response properties also pass --properties.

name: qm-multireference-check
description: Suspected multireference character (radicals, bond breaking, some TM complexes): run a FOD diagnostic before trusting single-reference DFT.
caveats: A high FOD (N_FOD) flags static correlation - single-reference DFT and even CCSD(T) may be unreliable, and SilicoLab has no multireference (CASSCF) engine.
Slow SCF convergence and a small HOMO-LUMO gap are corroborating hints.
body: The fractional-occupation-number-weighted density (FOD) is an easy DFT-based multireference indicator. Run it at a finite electronic temperature with tpss/def2-tzvp (the conventional FOD level). If N_FOD is large, treat single-reference energetics with caution.

name: qm-heavy-elements
description: Heavy elements (Z > 36, transition metals, lanthanides): the def2 basis family brings effective core potentials automatically; add --x2c only for explicit scalar relativity.
caveats: For Z > 36 the def2 family uses relativistic ECPs by construction - fine for most chemistry.
Use --x2c only when you need an explicit scalar-relativistic all-electron treatment (very heavy / 6th-row+ systems).
body: Transition-metal complexes often have multireference character - check `qm-multireference-check` (FOD) first. D4 dispersion is especially important for metal-organic and polarizable systems; add a -d4 suffix to a functional that lacks built-in dispersion.

name: qm-reference-energies
description: Benchmark / reference energies on a SMALL single-reference system: the MP2 -> CCSD -> CCSD(T) ladder with a large basis; CCSD(T) approaches gold standard.
caveats: CCSD(T) scales steeply (O(N^7)) - only for small molecules; DLPNO-CCSD(T) and W1/W2 are NOT available in SilicoLab.
Use as large a basis as you can afford (>= def2-tzvp, ideally def2-qzvp) for converged correlation; check the FOD diagnostic first - CCSD(T) assumes single-reference.
body: Coupled-cluster energies are reference values for single-reference systems. Pair a CCSD(T) energy with a wb97m-v/def2-qzvp DFT result to gauge the DFT error for your system. ri-mp2 (--ri-mp2) speeds up the MP2 step.

name: qm-solvation
description: Implicit solvent: add --smd <solvent> (e.g. --smd water) for SMD, or --solvent <name> for C-PCM. COSMO-RS / DCOSMO-RS are not available here.
caveats: SMD and C-PCM are continuum electrostatic models; COSMO-RS/DCOSMO-RS (best for solvation free energies) are not implemented in SilicoLab.
Gas-phase geometry + a solvent single point is usually enough; re-optimize in solvent only when the solvent is chemically involved.
body: For xtb-style fast solvation, --alpb/--gbsa exist but pair with semi-empirical parameters. For accurate aqueous free energies prefer --smd water on a good DFT geometry. At most one solvation model applies per job.

name: qm-periodic-dft
description: Periodic / crystal DFT on a unit cell: `qm periodic` (needs a defined cell). v1 is LDA/PADE only, GTH basis (SZV-GTH), Gamma or --kmesh; no charge/spin/relaxation.
caveats: Periodic v1 supports only LDA/PADE functionals and GTH basis sets - no hybrids, no geometry relaxation, no charge or spin.
Increase --kmesh and --cutoff for convergence; the structure must carry a unit cell (see `view cell`).
body: Periodic DFT is for crystalline / bulk systems. For a molecule in a box, use the molecular `qm` subcommands instead. Add --forces / --stress for the corresponding derivatives.
